Red panda

Small mammal native to the eastern Himalayas
Red panda
Conservation statusEndangered
Kingdom:Animalia
Phylum:Chordata
Family:Ailuridae
Genus:Ailurus

The red panda (Ailurus fulgens) is a small mammal native to the eastern Himalayas and southwestern China.

It has dense reddish-brown fur and a ringed tail, and is roughly the size of a domestic cat though with a longer body.

The red panda is largely herbivorous and feeds mainly on bamboo, supplementing its diet with fruit, acorns, and eggs.

It is a solitary and mostly nocturnal animal, sheltering in tree hollows during the day.

The species is classified as endangered, with fewer than 10,000 mature individuals remaining in the wild.
